mcqs.question: What is the main characteristic of static members in C++?

options.text: They are shared among all objects of the class
They are unique to each object instance
They cannot be accessed outside the class
They are automatically initialized to zero

mcqs.explanation: Static members are class-level variables that are shared across all instances of the class, unlike instance members which are unique to each object.

mcqs.question: How must static data members be initialized in C++?

options.text: Inside the class declaration using assignment
Outside the class using scope resolution operator
In the constructor of the class
Using the static keyword during declaration

mcqs.explanation: Static data members must be defined and initialized outside the class using the scope resolution operator (::), as they require separate storage allocation.

mcqs.question: Which of the following can static member functions access?

options.text: Both static and non-static data members
Only non-static data members
Only static data members
Neither static nor non-static data members

mcqs.explanation: Static member functions can only access static data members and other static member functions. They cannot access non-static members because they don't have access to the 'this' pointer.

mcqs.question: What happens if you try to access a non-static member from a static function?

options.text: It works fine if the object is passed as parameter
It causes a compilation error
It works but may cause runtime errors
It automatically becomes a static member

mcqs.explanation: Attempting to access non-static members from static functions causes a compilation error because static functions don't have access to the 'this' pointer needed to access instance-specific data.

mcqs.question: When is memory allocated for static data members?

options.text: When the first object is created
When the program starts execution
When the static member is first accessed
When the class is defined

mcqs.explanation: Memory for static data members is allocated when the program starts execution, before any objects are created. This is why they exist even when no objects of the class are instantiated.
